title: Nosebleed
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Nosebleed is bleeding from tissue lining the nose and is also called epistaxis.
Initial care includes direct pressure and avoiding nose picking or blowing after bleeding stops.
Medical evaluation is needed when bleeding is heavy, does not stop, follows injury, or occurs with concerning symptoms.
This source supports general patient education, not ED posterior-bleed risk stratification.
